channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: I.R.S. Prohibited From Pursuing Audits of Trump and His Family
item.description: As part of the Justice Department’s compensation fund deal, officials vowed not to pursue any matters, including those involving President Trump’s tax returns, that are pending.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer and Andrew Duehren
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The document was signed by acting Attorney General Todd Blanche.

item.title: The IRS Thought it Could Fight Trump’s Lawsuit, but it Struck a Deal Anyway
item.description: Officials wrote a memo outlining ways to challenge President Trump’s suit against the Internal Revenue Service. The administration is instead creating a compensation fund.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump sued the Internal Revenue Service in January, claiming that the agency had not done enough to prevent the leak of his tax information.

item.title: Iran War Exposes Shortcomings in U.S. Military Industrial Base
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is taking on a problem that the Pentagon and Congress have tried, and mostly failed, to address for years.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is attempting to take on the shortcomings in the Pentagon’s weapons procurement systems while pushing for a $1.5 trillion budget.

item.title: As a Weakened Putin Follows Trump to Beijing, Iran War Offers an Opening
item.description: With the upheaval in the Persian Gulf disrupting oil and gas supplies, Russia is looking to deepen its energy ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko and David Pierson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Vladimir Smirnov/Sputnik, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: In a photo released by Russian state media, President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia was met by China’s foreign minister, Wang Yi, after arriving in Beijing on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Says He Postponed a ‘Very Major Attack’ on Iran, Leaving Mideast on Edge
item.description: President Trump threatened “another big hit” on Iran, saying the country had days to return to the negotiating table, as Pakistan continued its mediation efforts to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump said the U.S. military was prepared for a “full, large scale assault” against Iran.

item.title: Worried About War’s Impact, Bond Investors Push Rates to Highest Level Since 2007
item.description: The 30-year U.S. Treasury yield hasn’t been this high since the lead-up to the global financial crisis. Across Europe and Asia, yields are also elevated.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Manager at Trump’s N.J. Golf Club Helped Plan Reflecting Pool Repairs
item.description: David Schutzenhofer, a Trump Organization employee, reached out to one of the vendors the government later hired for the iconic site near the Lincoln Memorial, documents show.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold and Maxine Joselow
item.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool has been plagued for decades by leaks and algae blooms, which various administrations have been unable to permanently fix.

item.title: Sean Duffy to Testify Amid Questions About Road Trip YouTube Series
item.description: The transportation secretary’s reality-show-like series is meant to commemorate the nation’s 250th birthday but has faced questions about its funding.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy may also face questions over how his department has addressed staffing shortages in air traffic control.

item.title: Trump’s Special Envoy to Greenland Receives a Cold Welcome From Locals
item.description: After President Trump’s threats to seize the island, Gov. Jeff Landry’s offers of MAGA hats and chocolate chip cookies fall flat.
item.dc:creator: Maya Tekeli and Jeffrey Gettleman
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Mark Schiefelbein/Associated Press
item.media:description: Gov. Jeff Landry of Louisiana at the White House last year. He is visiting Greenland this week as President Trump’s special envoy to the Danish territory.

item.title: A Generational Fracture Emerges for Republicans Over Foreign Policy
item.description: Younger Republicans are less likely to support overseas interventions and aid, a Times/Siena poll shows.
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Donald Trump and Tucker Carlson in 2024, when they were more in agreement.

item.title: Republican Senators Are Livid at Trump’s Endorsement of Paxton
item.description: News that President Trump had snubbed Senator John Cornyn of Texas, the four-term incumbent, was met with shock, anger and fear that the G.O.P. could lose his seat.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold, Megan Mineiro and Carl Hulse
item.media:credit: Jordan Vonderhaar for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator John Cornyn of Texas speaking with reporters on Monday after a campaign event in San Antonio.

item.title: What to Watch in Tuesday’s Primary Elections in Kentucky, Georgia and Beyond
item.description: A prominent Republican critic of President Trump is trying to hang on in Kentucky, and other states are also holding primaries that will test Mr. Trump’s power over his party.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Audra Melton for The New York Times
item.media:description: Georgia is holding competitive primary races for the Senate, governor and other offices on Tuesday.

item.title: The Best of ‘S.N.L.’ Season 51: Sensitive Strippers and Regretful Moms
item.description: This season “Saturday Night Live” found new ways to satirize the Trump administration and said goodbye to one of its most valuable cast members.
item.dc:creator: Dave Itzkoff
item.media:credit: Will Heath/NBC, via Getty Images
item.media:description: In a memorable “Saturday Night Live” sketch this season, a woman (Ashley Padilla, with Tommy Brennan and Jane Wickline) reconsidered her support for President Trump.

item.title: Before the San Diego Mosque Shooting, a Fearful Mother’s Phone Call Set Off a Frantic Search
item.description: San Diego police arrived to find three people dead. Nearby, they found two teenagers, the attackers, dead in a car. The violence shattered an idyllic Southern California city.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango, Christina Morales, Candice Reed and Chelsia Rose Marcius
item.category: Islamic Center of San Diego Shooting (2026)
item.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
item.media:description: Police officers raced around San Diego before converging at the mosque.

item.title: San Diego Mosque Shooting: What to Know About the Suspects and What Happened
item.description: Three men were killed in the attack at the Islamic Center of San Diego. Two teenage suspects were found dead in a car nearby, police said.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses and Chelsia Rose Marcius
item.category: Islamic Center of San Diego Shooting (2026)
item.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
item.media:description: Families escorting their children from the Islamic Center in San Diego, after a deadly shooting on Monday.

item.title: Justices Hint at Strains as Supreme Court Comes Under Scrutiny
item.description: Traveling across the country, justices defend the role of the court even as strained relations among its members emerge in writing and remarks.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow and Aishvarya Kavi
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
item.media:description: Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson of the Supreme Court talks with Judge Richard Gergel of the U.S. District Court in South Carolina during a fireside chat hosted by the American Law Institute in Washington on Monday.

item.title: How Trump Entrusted His World Cup to Another Giuliani
item.description: Andrew Giuliani, the son of Rudy and a longtime Trump loyalist, is overseeing American hosting duties for the World Cup next month. It’s a big promotion.
item.dc:creator: Matt Flegenheimer and Rebecca R. Ruiz
item.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times

item.title: ‘The Future of Truth’ Contains Quotes Made Up by A.I.
item.description: Steven Rosenbaum, author of “The Future of Truth,” said he had started his own investigation after The New York Times asked about the fake quotes.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin
item.category: The Future of Truth: How AI Reshapes Reality (Book)
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
item.media:description: OpenAI’s ChatGPT on a laptop. The rise of A.I. has set off fears among publishers that they may accidentally release books from authors who improperly use A.I.-generated language.

item.title: China Wants A.I. to Flourish, but Not at the Expense of Jobs
item.description: A series of precedent-setting rulings signals that Chinese courts are being enlisted to shield workers from displacement by artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
item.media:description: A robot learns to unpack and fold boxes at a lab working with Meituan in Shenzhen in January.

item.title: Powered by A.I., Google Changes Its Search Box for the First Time in 25 Years
item.description: Using a new Gemini A.I. model, the tech giant is overhauling its search box dimensions to answer longer queries, adding a video-generation tool and simplifying online shopping.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle, Kate Conger and Brian X. Chen
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: On Tuesday, Google said the A.I. shift had inspired it to overhaul its search bar for the first time since 2001.

item.title: How Google Is Starting to Win the A.I. Race
item.description: Despite its early stumbles, Google’s Gemini has leapfrogged ChatGPT in relevance and usefulness. Soon, it will be ubiquitous.
item.dc:creator: Brian X. Chen
item.media:credit: Sisi Yu

item.title: Woman Dies After Falling Into Open Manhole in Manhattan
item.description: The woman had parked her S.U.V., then got out and plunged 10 feet into the uncovered hole managed by Con Edison, prompting an investigation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Southall
item.category: Office of the Chief Medical Examiner of the City of New York
item.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
item.media:description: A 56-year-old woman died after she fell into an uncovered manhole near East 52nd Street and Fifth Avenue, one of the many hazards New Yorkers fear.

item.title: Tesla’s Semi Truck could Jolt the Trucking Industry
item.description: California truckers have expressed strong interest in the Tesla Semi because it costs much less and can travel further on a charge than electric trucks sold by established manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing and Gabriela Bhaskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Screens on either side of a Tesla Semi’s steering wheel provide a view of the traffic around the vehicle.

item.title: Amazon E-Bikes Take Root in Manhattan and Brooklyn
item.description: Small companies that handle the final delivery are relying on them to skirt New York City traffic. Amazon plans to try the bikes elsewhere, too.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis and Vincent Alban
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times

item.title: Bipartisan Bill Would Impose New Annual Fee on Electric Vehicles
item.description: A House transportation bill introduced this week would require owners of electric cars to pay $130 to cover the cost of road repairs.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
item.media:description: Congress is considering imposing a new annual fee on electric vehicles to help pay for road repairs.

item.title: L.I.R.R. Workers Are in Line for Raises, but at What Cost to New York?
item.description: Details are still emerging about the contract reached after a three-day strike that shut down America’s busiest passenger rail service. But the fallout could be felt for years.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen and Grace Ashford
item.media:credit: Anna Connors for The New York Times
item.media:description: Commuters at the Hicksville Long Island Rail Road station on Tuesday rode shuttle buses that were replacing train service disrupted by a rail workers’ strike.

item.title: Greg Hyman, Co-Creator of Tickle Me Elmo, Dies at 78
item.description: An electronics wizard, he was already a veteran inventor when he collaborated with Ron Dubren on an idea for a toy that giggled. The rest is retail history.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; The Strong National Museum of Play
item.media:description: Greg Hyman in 2012. The toy that he and Ron Dubren designed for Tyco not only giggled and spoke — “That tickles!” — but vibrated when tickled three times.

item.title: Tickle Me Elmo and the ‘Elmo-Mania’ That Consumed America
item.description: In 1996, the giggling stuffed doll turned holiday shopping into chaos, a preview of every sneaker drop, PlayStation launch and Taylor Swift ticket rush to come.
item.dc:creator: Amisha Padnani
item.media:credit: Steve Hart for The New York Times
item.media:description: There was a mad rush to grab one of the few remaining Tickle Me Elmo dolls at a Toys “R” Us store in Manhattan on Dec. 4, 1996.

item.title: How to End the Gerrymandering Doom Loop Forever
item.description: The political scientist Lee Drutman argues that we should switch to a system of proportional representation and put an end to our “trench warfare politics.”
item.dc:creator: Ezra Klein and Claire Gordon
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Ebola Outbreak in Central Africa Could Last Months, W.H.O. Says
item.description: Health officials reported more than 130 suspected deaths and 513 cases, a sharp rise since the outbreak was identified on May 15 in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and Yan Zhuang
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Jospin Mwisha/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A visitor has their temperature checked before entering Kyeshero Hospital in Goma on Monday.

item.title: Israeli Minister Threatens to Evict Palestinians From West Bank Hamlet
item.description: Bezalel Smotrich, the hard-line minister, said he was retaliating for the International Criminal Court prosecutor seeking his arrest, something the court did not confirm nor deny.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.media:credit: Erik Marmor/Getty Images
item.media:description: The Israeli finance minister, Bezalel Smotrich, in Jerusalem earlier this month.

item.title: Twist in FIFA Corruption Case: 2 Fugitives Emerge, Seeking a Plea Deal
item.description: Federal prosecutors are negotiating with Hugo and Mariano Jinkis, a father and son from Argentina, who after more than a decade suddenly agreed to meet in Brooklyn.
item.dc:creator: Ken Bensinger and Santul Nerkar
item.media:credit: Maxi Failla/Associated Press
item.media:description: Hugo and Mariano Jinkis surrendered in Argentina in 2015, but a judge there blocked their extradition. On Monday they began negotiating a possible plea with federal prosecutors in Brooklyn.
